---

# UC-19.2.26 · VxRail LCM bundle compliance and lifecycle drift

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We help you see when the Dell appliance software stack is on an old or split version between servers, and when the support window no longer lines up with what the machines are actually running, before that mismatch turns the next change into a long phone call. We keep the human owner’s name in the same picture so the right team is asked, not a random queue that only watches disks.*

---

## Description

Correlates VxRail Manager LCM state with Continuously Validated State bundle identity: compares `current_release_version` and staged bundle to governance targets, measures drift against the `vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog` and `vxrail_lcm_target_bundle` lookups, detects mixed ESXi or mixed-bundle nodes within one cluster, watches `lcm_state` and recent LCM history for failed operations, and surfaces `support_contract_status` so you treat lifecycle compliance and entitlement as one control—distinct from day-to-day vSAN and host health in UC-19.2.13.

## Value

VxRail LCM bundle compliance and lifecycle drift: when a cluster steps off the tested ESXi, firmware, and management-plane train Dell validates together, the next outage or one-off patch can land outside support policy even while hardware lights stay green. This use case makes bundle age, n-minus posture, staged-but-not-applied bundles, and mixed-node revision visible in one Splunk table so change owners, storage leadership, and vendor support all speak the same version language before a small drift becomes a contract or Sev-1 upgrade debate.

## Implementation

Provision `index=vxrail` and `index=vmware` (or equivalent), HEC token, a Python poller against `/rest/vxm/v1/lcm` plus sibling endpoints on a 2–5 minute schedule for LCM and hosts, daily or weekly for history, and two versioned CSV lookups. Save the `spl` as `ss_uc_19_2_26_vxrail_lcm_compliance` on a 15–30m schedule with 60–90m per-cluster throttle for sustained `medium` or `low` rows, immediate route for `critical` mixed with freeze logic as written. Map alert actions to the change and platform owners named in the lookup, not the storage-only queue, and attach `cluster_id`, `current_release_version`, `target_release_version`, `n_minus_actual`, and `support_contract_status` to tickets.

## Evidence

A scheduled saved search in your monitoring app over `index=vxrail` and `index=vmware` using the seven `vxrail:vxm:*` HEC families plus `vmware:perf`, the two CSV lookups in version control, and 13+ months of online retention. Evidence rows include `current_release_version`, `target_release_version` from the governance lookup, `n_minus_actual`, `bundle_age_days`, `lcm_state`, and `support_contract_status` for audit and vendor bridge calls.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab index, post `vxrail:vxm:lcm:status` with `lcm_state=Failed` for `cluster_id=C1` and `current_release_version=8.0.100-OLD`; add matching `vxrail:vxm:cluster` rows, `vxrail:vxm:host` with two different `esxi_version` strings for the same `cluster_id`, and lookup CSV rows that set `target_bundle_n_minus=0` and catalog rows with `is_supported_until_epoch` in the past for that bundle. The saved search should return `mixed_bundle_flag=1`, `sev0` of `high` for mixed bundle, `out_of_sup=1` driving `critical` when failed_lcm=0, and a combined `severity_bucket` that reflects freeze rules when you add `change_freeze_until` in the future for the same `cluster_id`.

### Negative scenario

Ingest a steady healthy cluster: one `esxi_version` on all host rows, `lcm_state=Idle`, catalog `is_supported_until_epoch` one year in the future, and `n_minus_actual` at or under `target_bundle_n_minus`; the primary row should land in `ok` and disappear from a `where severity_bucket!="ok"` alert, while the optional reporting search without that filter still lists the row for green dashboards. Add `change_freeze_until` two weeks ahead: a synthetic `high` from an intentionally stale bundle in a second fixture should demote to `low` or `ok` if you temporarily force sev0 high without `out_of_sup` or `failed_lcm`, matching the `freeze_active` pass.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
You need a Heavy Forwarder or build agent on a management network that can open HTTPS to the VxRail Manager VIP on port 443, run Python 3.10+ with the `requests` module, and post newline-delimited JSON to Splunk HEC. Read-only service accounts are preferred; if you use a personal admin, rotate the secret when people leave. NTP on VxRail Manager, vCenter, and the poller must agree within a few seconds or `_time` skew will make LCM “Failed” and “InProgress” look mis-ordered. There is no Splunk-supported TA on Splunkbase that polls `/rest/vxm/v1/lcm` and publishes these fields; acknowledge that in writing to auditors so they do not search Splunkbase for a magic install. The Continuously Validated State (CVS) bundle is the VxRail-specific guarantee: the same build string ties together VxRail software, ESXi build, HBA, NIC, and drive firmware, iDRAC or BMC, SCG, and the VxRail Manager service plane. A mixed-bundle cluster, where one node is still on bundle N-2 while another is on N, is a compliance gap even if every LED is green and vSAN health is perfect—support and upgrade sequencing depend on one coherent train per cluster, applied through VxRail Manager LCM, not ad-hoc ISO mounts. The Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380) is required only for `vmware:perf` and optional `vmware:vc:event` to sanity-check ESXi build and vLCM events; the canonical truth for the bundle is still VxRail Manager. Dell SupportAssist and SRS are tertiary: in incidents you will still log into Dell to confirm the service tag, but the Splunk use case is REST plus lookups, not a full SRS ETL. Two customer-owned lookup files in `lookups/` (Search Head) are mandatory: (1) `vxrail_lcm_target_bundle.csv` with columns `cluster_id`, `cluster_name`, `target_bundle_version`, `target_bundle_release_date_epoch`, `target_bundle_n_minus` (the maximum number of released bundles behind current GA you allow, for example 1 for production and 3 for some lab clusters), `business_service`, `owner_email`, `change_freeze_until` in ISO-8601 or a date-only string Splunk can parse, and (2) `vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog.csv` with `bundle_version`, `release_date_epoch`, `is_ga` (0/1), `is_supported_until_epoch`, `expected_esxi_build` (or build token your org maps from Dell tables), `expected_iDRAC_min_version`, and `notes`. Add optional `bundle_seq` or `rank` monotonically increasing with each GA to make `n_minus_actual` deterministic; the sample SPL can read it when present, and you should refresh the catalog at least as often as Dell issues new VxRay Software bundles, historically about one GA train every two to three months. Publish transforms `vxrail_lcm_target_bundle` and `vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog` and restrict read roles because owner email and `business_service` are sensitive. Keep the poller and HEC details in a private repo; never check API secrets into the catalogue Git.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Build a poller that authenticates the way your API guide states (local Basic for some builds, session cookie after `POST /rest/vxm/v1/system/initialize` in others, or a vendor header such as `X-Vxm-Token` after a token call). Re-auth on `401/403` and back off on `429` with jitter; never tight-loop 429 storms during large LCM. For each VxRay Manager, poll `GET /rest/vxm/v1/lcm` and emit one HEC line per cluster to `sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:lcm:status` with a flat JSON that includes: `current_release_version`, `staged_release_version`, `release_repository_url`, `lcm_state`, `last_lcm_operation_time`, `last_lcm_operation_status`, and `current_health_status`. A representative JSON object (synthetic) looks like: {"cluster_id":"a1b2c3d4-...","cluster_name":"prod-vx1","current_release_version":"8.0.200-00001234","staged_release_version":"8.0.220-00005678","lcm_state":"StagedReady","last_lcm_operation_status":"Success","current_health_status":"Healthy"} — you may emit camelCase if that matches the API; the SPL `coalesce` list handles both. Poll `GET /rest/vxm/v1/lcm/history` on a 15–60 minute cadence and take the last 24–48h window into HEC with `sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:lcm:history` including `operation_id`, `start_time`, `end_time`, `status`, `bundle_version`, and any `error_message` text. Poll `GET /rest/vxm/v1/lcm/precheck` when LCM is not idle, or on a short timer during change windows, to `sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:lcm:precheck` with enough text fields to know whether a warning is blocking apply. Use `GET /rest/vxm/v1/cluster` for `vxrail:vxm:cluster` to capture `vc_uuid`, `name`, and `current_release_version` to cross-check LCM. Use `GET /rest/vxm/v1/hosts` (or the documented cluster-hosts path) for per-node `esxi_version`, `bios_version`, `bmc_version`, and firmware multivalue fields on `vxrail:vxm:host`—one event per node or one batched object with `cluster_id` repeated; both work if the SPL sees node rows. `GET /rest/vxm/v1/supportability/status` lands on `vxrail:vxm:supportability` with `support_contract_status` and `expiration_date`. HEC: create an acknowledgement-enabled token, place it in `local/inputs.conf` on a Heavy Forwarder, and map sourcetypes with `index = vxrail`, `sourcetype` per stanza, `SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false`, `TRUNCATE = 2000000`, and `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json` in `props.conf` on the first parsing tier. Example global Python wrapper sketch: authenticate, issue each GET, wrap body under `json`, set `_time` to the poll time for bundle status and to API start time for large history if present. On the vSphere side, enable Splunk add-on 5380 for vCenter, route `sourcetype=vmware:perf` to `index=vmware` (or your metrics index) and, if you use events for vLCM, `sourcetype=vmware:vc:event`. If `vmware:perf` lacks `cluster_id`, add a poller post-step to enrich the ESXi FQDN with a small CSV `host_to_cluster.csv` in the same app until a universal field exists—document that in your local runbook, not in this file’s SPL. The bundle catalog must be re-imported or copied into `lookups/` on a change ticket whenever Dell posts a new GA, because the severity ladder compares `is_supported_until_epoch` and `release_date_epoch` to wall-clock. Step 1 ends with: two CSVs versioned, HEC and TA installed, and a smoke test cURL to each REST path with `200` and JSON.

### Step 2 — Create search and alert
Copy the `spl` field from this use case in full; the leading `` `comment` `` token is the audit id. The query opens with `| multisearch` to union seven pre-aggregated subsearches, each with `earliest` on its base: `earliest=-2h@h` for hot LCM, cluster, host, precheck, supportability, and `vmware:perf` because those update quickly; `earliest=-24h@h` for LCM history so failure counts are meaningful. After each subsearch, the pipeline `stats` once by `cluster_id` so you never count duplicate poll minutes twice. The first `| join` brings governance from `| inputlookup vxrail_lcm_target_bundle` on `cluster_id` to add `target_release_version` (from `target_bundle_version`), `target_bundle_n_minus`, `business_service`, `owner_email`, and `change_freeze_until`. The second join ties `current_release_version` to `vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog` as `current_release_version = bundle_key` to pull `release_date_epoch`, `is_supported_until_epoch`, and optional `cur_seq` if you add `bundle_seq` to the CSV. A third, parameterless, join subsearch pulls a single `ga_seq` as the current GA step from the same catalog. `bundle_age_days` is `( now() - release_date_epoch ) / 86400` when `release_date_epoch` is populated. `n_minus_actual` is the integer gap when `ga_seq` and `cur_seq` are both present; otherwise it stays `0` and you reason about drift using `is_supported_until_epoch` and LCM state until you finish the catalog. The `sev0` `case` ladder (uppercase AND/OR in every multi-clause `eval` you extend) is: `critical` when the bundle is past `is_supported_until_epoch` (out of support) or `lcm_state` matches `Failed` case-insensitively; `high` when more than one distinct `esxi_version` exists in the 2h host window or when `n_minus_actual` exceeds the governed `target_bundle_n_minus` number; `medium` when `bundle_age_days` is above twelve months (tunable); `low` when precheck is flagged or a recent LCM history failure count is non-zero. Anything else is `ok`. The freeze demotion: if `change_freeze_until` is still in the future, map `sev0` of `critical` to `medium` and `high` to `low` in `severity_bucket` so approved quiet periods suppress paging noise; re-run a reporting copy without freeze if you need to see the raw `sev0` for audits. In alert setup, schedule 15 or 30 minutes, window last 2h for the hot arms, 24h for history, trigger when `severity_bucket` is in the list you care about, throttle 60–90 minutes on `cluster_id+severity_bucket` for `medium` and `low` noise, and do not throttle the first `critical` on a new `cluster_id`. Send HTTP to ServiceNow with fields: `cluster_id`, `current_release_version`, `target_release_version` or pending staged bundle, `n_minus_actual`, `target_bundle_n_minus`, and `support_contract_status`. Wire email or Slack to the LCM and change team named in the lookup, not a generic “storage L2” queue. The SPL block for copy-paste is the exact `spl` property without edits:

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.26 — VxRail LCM bundle compliance, mixed-bundle drift, support entitlement. SOURCES: vxrail:vxm:lcm:status, vxrail:vxm:lcm:history, vxrail:vxm:lcm:precheck, vxrail:vxm:cluster, vxrail:vxm:host, vxrail:vxm:supportability, vmware:perf; LOOKUPS: vxrail_lcm_target_bundle, vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:status" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval s_cur=coalesce(current_release_version, currentReleaseVersion, bundle_version, bundleVersion, "")
      | eval s_stg=coalesce(staged_release_version, stagedReleaseVersion, "")
      | eval s_lcm=coalesce(lcm_state, lcmState, state, "")
      | eval s_lops=coalesce(last_lcm_operation_status, lastLcmOperationStatus, "")
      | eval s_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, "")
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(s_cur) as current_release_version latest(s_stg) as staged_release_version latest(s_lcm) as lcm_state latest(s_lops) as last_lcm_operation_status latest(s_name) as cluster_name by cluster_id
      | eval st="A" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:history" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval sstat=coalesce(status, Status, messageStatus, "")
      | eval is_fail=if(match(tostring(sstat), "(?i)fail"), 1, 0)
      | stats max(_time) as _t sum(is_fail) as hist_fail_24h by cluster_id
      | eval st="B" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:precheck" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval wtxt=toString(coalesce(error_message, errorMessage, warning, Warning, precheck_text, precheckText, ""))
      | eval pchk=if((len(wtxt)>0) OR (match(tostring(overall), "(?i)warn|fail")), 1, 0)
      | stats max(_time) as _t max(pchk) as precheck_flag by cluster_id
      | eval st="C" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:cluster" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval c_rel=coalesce(current_release_version, currentReleaseVersion, "")
      | eval cnm=coalesce(name, cluster_name, clusterName, "")
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(c_rel) as cluster_api_release latest(cnm) as cluster_name_c by cluster_id
      | eval st="D" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:host" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval s_esx=toString(coalesce(esxi_version, esxiVersion, product_version, ""))
      | where len(cluster_id)>0
      | stats max(_time) as _t dc(s_esx) as distinct_esxi_versions by cluster_id
      | eval mixed_bundle_flag=if((distinct_esxi_versions>1), 1, 0)
      | eval st="E" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:supportability" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, support_cluster_id, "")
      | eval sup=toString(coalesce(support_contract_status, supportContractStatus, service_contract_status, ""))
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(sup) as support_contract_status by cluster_id
      | eval st="F" ]
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci OR index=vxrail) sourcetype="vmware:perf" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(vxrail_cluster_id, mhl, cluster, clusterName, host_cluster_id, "")
      | eval perf_b=toString(coalesce(os_version, product_version, productVersion, guestFullName, ""))
      | where len(tostring(cluster_id))>0
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(perf_b) as perf_product_hint by cluster_id
      | eval st="G" ]
| eval _time=coalesce(_t, _time)
| eval cluster_id=if(isnotnull(cluster_id) AND (len(tostring(cluster_id))>0), tostring(cluster_id), "")
| where len(cluster_id)>0
| stats
    max(_time) as _time
    max(st) as st_src
    max(current_release_version) as current_release_version
    max(staged_release_version) as staged_release_version
    max(lcm_state) as lcm_state
    max(last_lcm_operation_status) as last_lcm_operation_status
    max(cluster_name) as cluster_name_a
    max(cluster_name_c) as cluster_name_c
    max(cluster_api_release) as cluster_api_release
    max(hist_fail_24h) as hist_fail_24h
    max(precheck_flag) as precheck_flag
    max(mixed_bundle_flag) as mixed_bundle_flag
    max(support_contract_status) as support_contract_status
    max(perf_product_hint) as perf_product_hint
    max(distinct_esxi_versions) as distinct_esxi_versions
  by cluster_id
| eval current_release_version=if(isnotnull(current_release_version) AND (len(tostring(current_release_version))>0), tostring(current_release_version), tostring(coalesce(cluster_api_release, "")))
| eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(cluster_name_a) AND (len(tostring(cluster_name_a))>0), tostring(cluster_name_a), if(isnotnull(cluster_name_c) AND (len(tostring(cluster_name_c))>0), tostring(cluster_name_c), tostring(cluster_id)))
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup vxrail_lcm_target_bundle
     | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
     | eval target_release_version=toString(coalesce(target_bundle_version, targetVersion, declared_target, ""))
     | eval target_bundle_n_minus=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_bundle_n_minus, n_minus_allowed, "99")), 10)
     | eval business_service=toString(coalesce(business_service, businessService, ""))
     | eval owner_email=toString(coalesce(owner_email, ownerEmail, ""))
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, changeFreezeUntil, ""))
     | fields + cluster_id, target_release_version, target_bundle_n_minus, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until ]
| join type=left max=0 current_release_version
    [| inputlookup vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog
     | eval bundle_key=toString(coalesce(bundle_version, BundleVersion, ""))
     | eval release_date_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(release_date_epoch, releaseDateEpoch, "0")), 10)
     | eval is_supported_until_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_supported_until_epoch, supportedUntilEpoch, "0")), 10)
     | eval expected_esxi_build=toString(coalesce(expected_esxi_build, expectedEsxiBuild, ""))
     | eval is_ga=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_ga, isGA, 1)), 10)
     | eval cur_seq=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(bundle_seq, rank, 0)), 10)
     | eval current_release_version=bundle_key
     | fields + current_release_version, release_date_epoch, is_supported_until_epoch, expected_esxi_build, is_ga, cur_seq ]
| join type=left max=0 [ | inputlookup vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog | eval is_ga2=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_ga, isGA, 1)), 10) | where is_ga2=1 | sort 0 - release_date_epoch | head 1 | eval ga_seq=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(bundle_seq, rank, 0)), 10) | fields + ga_seq ]
| fillnull value=0 hist_fail_24h precheck_flag mixed_bundle_flag
| fillnull value=1 distinct_esxi_versions
| eval bundle_age_days=if((release_date_epoch>0), ((now() - (release_date_epoch)) / 86400), 0)
| eval n_minus_actual=if((isnotnull(ga_seq)) AND (isnotnull(cur_seq)), if(((ga_seq) - (cur_seq))<0, 0, (ga_seq) - (cur_seq)), 0)
| eval is_sup_ok=if((isnotnull(is_supported_until_epoch)) AND (is_supported_until_epoch>0), 1, 0)
| eval out_of_sup=if((is_sup_ok=1) AND ((is_supported_until_epoch) < (now())), 1, 0)
| eval failed_lcm=if(match(tostring(lcm_state), "(?i)^[[:space:]]*Failed[[:space:]]*$"), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    (out_of_sup=1) OR (failed_lcm=1), "critical",
    (mixed_bundle_flag=1) OR ((n_minus_actual) > (target_bundle_n_minus)), "high",
    (bundle_age_days) > 365, "medium",
    (precheck_flag=1) OR (hist_fail_24h>0), "low",
    1=1, "ok" )
| eval cfti=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cfti)) OR (isnotnull(cft2)) OR (isnotnull(cft3)) OR (isnotnull(cft4)), if((now()) < coalesce(cfti, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0), 1, 0), 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1), case((sev0="critical"), "medium", (sev0="high"), "low", 1=1, sev0), sev0)
| table cluster_id, cluster_name, current_release_version, target_release_version, n_minus_actual, target_bundle_n_minus, bundle_age_days, lcm_state, mixed_bundle_flag, distinct_esxi_versions, support_contract_status, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
```

Severity and freeze explanation for operators: a row can be `high` for mixed-bundle for two different reasons: half the nodes still rebooting in the middle of an LCM (expected briefly) or a true operational mistake such as a hotfix install outside the bundle (bad until rolled back). Bundle drift severity also rises with calendar age, but a bundle that is only six months old yet past `is_supported_until_epoch` is worse than a fifteen-month-old bundle still inside Dell support—`sev0` is `critical` for `out_of_sup` or failed LCM before the freeze pass. When a freeze is active, `critical` and `high` are demoted one step for alert routing; keep a non-freeze scheduled report for audit if you need the pre-demotion `sev0`.

### Step 3 — Validate
On day one, run three short searches. (1) `index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:status" OR sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:cluster") earliest=-2h@h | stats latest(_raw) as ex by cluster_id sourcetype` — you should see one meaningful JSON line per cluster per sourcetype in the last poll, not zero. (2) `| inputlookup vxrail_lcm_target_bundle | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id) | table cluster_id target_bundle_version target_bundle_n_minus` — the join keys are human-readable, not `NULL` for production clusters. (3) `index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:history" earliest=-24h@h | timechart count by status` — shows history is reaching Splunk, even if the count is small in a quiet day. (4) Spot-check: compare `current_release_version` in Splunk to VxRay Manager LCM, and `last_lcm_operation_status` to the last action in the UI. If the numbers disagree while `lcm_state` is `InProgress` or a node is in maintenance, wait for `lcm_state=Idle` per vendor behavior before you declare a data bug.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboards: a cluster-versus-bundle heatmap, a “mixed cluster” list driven by `mixed_bundle_flag` and `distinct_esxi_versions`, a per-node firmware drift bar (child search on `vxrail:vxm:host` with `values(bios_version)` and `values(bmc_version)` for the chosen `cluster_id`), a horizontal timeline of LCM history for failures, and a gauge of days to support `expiration_date` for SupportAssist. Drills: token-pass `cluster_id` into the UC-19.2.13 health dashboard for host and vSAN context without merging the SPL, and pass `host_name` to vCenter. Alert routing: send `critical` to the platform and change lead in `owner_email` plus the ITSM service tied to `business_service`, not a pure storage on-call unless that team really owns the VxRay LCM runbook. ITSI: map an entity for each `cluster_id` with two KPIs: LCM `severity_bucket` as a 0–4 score and a boolean `entitlement_risk` from `support_contract_status` plus `is_supported_until_epoch` math in a child search. Link runbook “VXR-LCM-REM-01 — schedule a validated maintenance window, download bundle, precheck, apply with witness acknowledgements” in the ServiceNow work note template. Standard ticket short description: “VxRay LCM drift — {cluster_id} {current_release_version} target {target_release_version} n-minus {n_minus_actual} contract {support_contract_status}”.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
Session timeout: if the poller uses a short-lived VxRay Manager token, every eight or twelve hours you will see 401s and empty indexes; re-auth and rotate secrets; do not set alert thresholds to “zero events means green.” `current_release_version` mismatch between `vxrail:vxm:lcm:status` and `vxrail:vxm:cluster` during a partial apply is expected: wait for `lcm_state=Idle` and compare again; if they never converge, open a VxRay case with both JSON blobs. Per-node ESXi or firmware skew after a manual hotfix: confirm with the host view, then roll back with Dell guidance or re-run the bundle in LCM; this UC will keep flagging `mixed_bundle_flag` until the cluster is back on one train. Stale `vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog.csv`: the CSV is your responsibility; when Splunk says `out_of_sup=1` and Dell’s site says extended support, your epoch fields are simply wrong. Mixed-bundle after a failed mid-cluster LCM: the history sourcetype will show a failed `operation_id`; re-stage and finish with a maintenance window, do not “fix” the alert in Splunk alone. HEC issues: 404 on collector path means wrong `services/collector/event` URL; 400 with “invalid data” is usually a missing `event` key wrapper; 503 on the indexer is back-pressure from large JSON—raise `TRUNCATE` and post smaller fragments. vCenter perf without `cluster_id` leaves `perf_product_hint` blank; the row is still about VxRay truth—fix tagging before you use perf as a gate. vLCM: optional `vmware:vc:event` with keywords `vLCM` or `remediat` to complement but not override VxRay states. SupportAssist: open Dell SRS in parallel when `support_contract_status` is `Expired` to capture entitlement even though SRS is not in the SPL. Finally, re-read the freeze column quarterly so `change_freeze_until` is not a stale year-9999 that suppresses all operational alerts.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.26 — VxRail LCM bundle compliance, mixed-bundle drift, support entitlement. SOURCES: vxrail:vxm:lcm:status, vxrail:vxm:lcm:history, vxrail:vxm:lcm:precheck, vxrail:vxm:cluster, vxrail:vxm:host, vxrail:vxm:supportability, vmware:perf; LOOKUPS: vxrail_lcm_target_bundle, vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:status" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval s_cur=coalesce(current_release_version, currentReleaseVersion, bundle_version, bundleVersion, "")
      | eval s_stg=coalesce(staged_release_version, stagedReleaseVersion, "")
      | eval s_lcm=coalesce(lcm_state, lcmState, state, "")
      | eval s_lops=coalesce(last_lcm_operation_status, lastLcmOperationStatus, "")
      | eval s_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, "")
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(s_cur) as current_release_version latest(s_stg) as staged_release_version latest(s_lcm) as lcm_state latest(s_lops) as last_lcm_operation_status latest(s_name) as cluster_name by cluster_id
      | eval st="A" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:history" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval sstat=coalesce(status, Status, messageStatus, "")
      | eval is_fail=if(match(tostring(sstat), "(?i)fail"), 1, 0)
      | stats max(_time) as _t sum(is_fail) as hist_fail_24h by cluster_id
      | eval st="B" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:lcm:precheck" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval wtxt=toString(coalesce(error_message, errorMessage, warning, Warning, precheck_text, precheckText, ""))
      | eval pchk=if((len(wtxt)>0) OR (match(tostring(overall), "(?i)warn|fail")), 1, 0)
      | stats max(_time) as _t max(pchk) as precheck_flag by cluster_id
      | eval st="C" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:cluster" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval c_rel=coalesce(current_release_version, currentReleaseVersion, "")
      | eval cnm=coalesce(name, cluster_name, clusterName, "")
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(c_rel) as cluster_api_release latest(cnm) as cluster_name_c by cluster_id
      | eval st="D" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:host" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, "")
      | eval s_esx=toString(coalesce(esxi_version, esxiVersion, product_version, ""))
      | where len(cluster_id)>0
      | stats max(_time) as _t dc(s_esx) as distinct_esxi_versions by cluster_id
      | eval mixed_bundle_flag=if((distinct_esxi_versions>1), 1, 0)
      | eval st="E" ]
    [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:supportability" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, support_cluster_id, "")
      | eval sup=toString(coalesce(support_contract_status, supportContractStatus, service_contract_status, ""))
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(sup) as support_contract_status by cluster_id
      | eval st="F" ]
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci OR index=vxrail) sourcetype="vmware:perf" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(vxrail_cluster_id, mhl, cluster, clusterName, host_cluster_id, "")
      | eval perf_b=toString(coalesce(os_version, product_version, productVersion, guestFullName, ""))
      | where len(tostring(cluster_id))>0
      | stats max(_time) as _t latest(perf_b) as perf_product_hint by cluster_id
      | eval st="G" ]
| eval _time=coalesce(_t, _time)
| eval cluster_id=if(isnotnull(cluster_id) AND (len(tostring(cluster_id))>0), tostring(cluster_id), "")
| where len(cluster_id)>0
| stats
    max(_time) as _time
    max(st) as st_src
    max(current_release_version) as current_release_version
    max(staged_release_version) as staged_release_version
    max(lcm_state) as lcm_state
    max(last_lcm_operation_status) as last_lcm_operation_status
    max(cluster_name) as cluster_name_a
    max(cluster_name_c) as cluster_name_c
    max(cluster_api_release) as cluster_api_release
    max(hist_fail_24h) as hist_fail_24h
    max(precheck_flag) as precheck_flag
    max(mixed_bundle_flag) as mixed_bundle_flag
    max(support_contract_status) as support_contract_status
    max(perf_product_hint) as perf_product_hint
    max(distinct_esxi_versions) as distinct_esxi_versions
  by cluster_id
| eval current_release_version=if(isnotnull(current_release_version) AND (len(tostring(current_release_version))>0), tostring(current_release_version), tostring(coalesce(cluster_api_release, "")))
| eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(cluster_name_a) AND (len(tostring(cluster_name_a))>0), tostring(cluster_name_a), if(isnotnull(cluster_name_c) AND (len(tostring(cluster_name_c))>0), tostring(cluster_name_c), tostring(cluster_id)))
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup vxrail_lcm_target_bundle
     | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
     | eval target_release_version=toString(coalesce(target_bundle_version, targetVersion, declared_target, ""))
     | eval target_bundle_n_minus=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_bundle_n_minus, n_minus_allowed, "99")), 10)
     | eval business_service=toString(coalesce(business_service, businessService, ""))
     | eval owner_email=toString(coalesce(owner_email, ownerEmail, ""))
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, changeFreezeUntil, ""))
     | fields + cluster_id, target_release_version, target_bundle_n_minus, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until ]
| join type=left max=0 current_release_version
    [| inputlookup vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog
     | eval bundle_key=toString(coalesce(bundle_version, BundleVersion, ""))
     | eval release_date_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(release_date_epoch, releaseDateEpoch, "0")), 10)
     | eval is_supported_until_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_supported_until_epoch, supportedUntilEpoch, "0")), 10)
     | eval expected_esxi_build=toString(coalesce(expected_esxi_build, expectedEsxiBuild, ""))
     | eval is_ga=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_ga, isGA, 1)), 10)
     | eval cur_seq=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(bundle_seq, rank, 0)), 10)
     | eval current_release_version=bundle_key
     | fields + current_release_version, release_date_epoch, is_supported_until_epoch, expected_esxi_build, is_ga, cur_seq ]
| join type=left max=0 [ | inputlookup vxrail_supported_bundle_catalog | eval is_ga2=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_ga, isGA, 1)), 10) | where is_ga2=1 | sort 0 - release_date_epoch | head 1 | eval ga_seq=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(bundle_seq, rank, 0)), 10) | fields + ga_seq ]
| fillnull value=0 hist_fail_24h precheck_flag mixed_bundle_flag
| fillnull value=1 distinct_esxi_versions
| eval bundle_age_days=if((release_date_epoch>0), ((now() - (release_date_epoch)) / 86400), 0)
| eval n_minus_actual=if((isnotnull(ga_seq)) AND (isnotnull(cur_seq)), if(((ga_seq) - (cur_seq))<0, 0, (ga_seq) - (cur_seq)), 0)
| eval is_sup_ok=if((isnotnull(is_supported_until_epoch)) AND (is_supported_until_epoch>0), 1, 0)
| eval out_of_sup=if((is_sup_ok=1) AND ((is_supported_until_epoch) < (now())), 1, 0)
| eval failed_lcm=if(match(tostring(lcm_state), "(?i)^[[:space:]]*Failed[[:space:]]*$"), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    (out_of_sup=1) OR (failed_lcm=1), "critical",
    (mixed_bundle_flag=1) OR ((n_minus_actual) > (target_bundle_n_minus)), "high",
    (bundle_age_days) > 365, "medium",
    (precheck_flag=1) OR (hist_fail_24h>0), "low",
    1=1, "ok" )
| eval cfti=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cfti)) OR (isnotnull(cft2)) OR (isnotnull(cft3)) OR (isnotnull(cft4)), if((now()) < coalesce(cfti, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0), 1, 0), 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1), case((sev0="critical"), "medium", (sev0="high"), "low", 1=1, sev0), sev0)
| table cluster_id, cluster_name, current_release_version, target_release_version, n_minus_actual, target_bundle_n_minus, bundle_age_days, lcm_state, mixed_bundle_flag, distinct_esxi_versions, support_contract_status, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
```

## Visualization

Row 1: a matrix heatmap of `cluster_id` (rows) and observed bundle (columns) with color by `severity_bucket`, plus a single-value of clusters in `critical`. Row 2: a panel that flags `mixed_bundle_flag=1` with a bar of `distinct_esxi_versions` per cluster. Row 3: a timeline of LCM `hist_fail_24h` and annotations from precheck. Row 4: a gauge of days-to-support `expiration` when `support_contract_status` is present. Drills: raw `vxrail:vxm:lcm:history` for a cluster, and the corresponding vCenter cluster view. Pair with the UC-19.2.13 dashboard but keep LCM on its own row so operators do not confuse operational health with lifecycle entitlement.

## Known False Positives

A planned LCM change during a `change_freeze_until` window can still show staged versus current in Splunk; the demotion in the SPL and the CAB record together explain a yellow row that is not a process gap. A mixed-bundle or mixed-ESXi state during a rolling mid-cluster LCM is normal for hours and should be evaluated against `lcm_state` in {`InProgress`, `StagedReady`} before paging as if it were steady-state split-brain. A precheck that Dell documents as an accepted interoperability warning can remain `low` for quarters; the text column in the poller, not the boolean alone, tells you whether the finding is new. Support contract `expiration_date` can lag a renewal already signed on paper until SupportAssist or SRS reflects it—treat a one-day “expired” with an open P1 to Dell with the PO number, not an automatic rebuild. vCenter `vmware:perf` that lacks `cluster_id` because the forwarder is not yet tagging hosts yields empty `perf_product_hint` while LCM is still valid; that is a collection gap, not an automatic compliance miss. A fresh GA bundle in the field before your catalog CSV is updated can overstate n-minus; refresh the catalog every Dell publish cycle, roughly quarterly.

## References

- [Dell VxRail documentation (LCM, API, and support)](https://www.dell.com/support/manuals/en-us/vxrail-appliance-series-docs)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)
- [Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/)
